requirements.summary: Build a responsive marketing site with top navigation and Home/About/Services/Contact sections.
acceptanceCriteria: Site displays the business name “Jo enterprises” prominently in the header/hero.
Navigation links allow moving to Home, About, Services, and Contact (via pages or anchored sections).
Layout is mobile-first and remains usable on common breakpoints (mobile/tablet/desktop).

file_operations.description: Create the main marketing page layout using anchored sections (Home hero + value proposition, About, Services, Contact) and a responsive top navigation that links to each section.
Create a responsive header with logo, business name, and keyboard-navigable anchor links to Home/About/Services/Contact (mobile-first layout with a compact menu for small screens).
Create presentational section components for Home (hero + value proposition), About, and Services with semantic headings and responsive spacing.
Ensure the app renders the new App component and that global styles are imported (App is expected at frontend/src/App.tsx).
Verify document title and language metadata remain correct for an English marketing site and align any visible name/title text with “Jo enterprises”.

requirements.summary: Define and apply a professional, consistent theme (non-blue/purple primary palette) across the site.
acceptanceCriteria: A consistent set of typography styles (headings/body), spacing rules, and button/link styles is used across the site.
The theme avoids a default/template look by using a distinct color palette and clear hierarchy (without using blue/purple as the primary palette).

file_operations.description: Create global styles including Tailwind base layers, site-wide typography defaults, focus-visible styles, and CSS variables for a distinct non-blue/purple primary palette used consistently across components.
Adjust Tailwind theme extensions (e.g., radii, typography defaults, and color tokens via CSS variables) to support a cohesive business theme with a non-blue/purple primary palette.
Add reusable marketing primitives (e.g., Container, SectionHeading, PrimaryButton, TextLink) to enforce consistent spacing, typography, and component styling across the site.
Apply the theme primitives and consistent layout/typography across all sections to avoid a default/template look.

requirements.summary: Add a validated Contact form that submits inquiries to the backend and shows success/error states.
acceptanceCriteria: Form validates required fields and shows clear English error messages.
On successful submit, the user sees a success confirmation state and the form resets.
Failed submissions show an error state without losing the user’s typed message.

file_operations.description: Create the Contact section containing a form (name, email, message) with client-side validation, accessible labels, clear English error messages, and success/error submission states.
Create a React Query mutation hook that uses useActor to call the backend capability submitContactForm(name, email, message) and returns mutation state for success/error UI.
Wire the ContactSection into the Contact anchor section and ensure submission success resets the form while failures preserve the typed message.

requirements.summary: Add a site-wide footer and baseline accessibility essentials (contrast, headings, focus, keyboard navigation, labeled fields).
acceptanceCriteria: Footer is present across the site and includes “Jo enterprises”.
All interactive elements are reachable via keyboard and have visible focus styles.
Text contrast is sufficient for readability and form fields have associated labels.

file_operations.description: Create a footer component with “Jo enterprises” and a short English tagline, styled consistently with the site theme.
Add/verify visible focus styles (e.g., :focus-visible) and ensure default text/background contrast supports readability across the theme.
Render the Footer across the site and verify headings structure (H1 in hero, logical H2/H3 in sections) and keyboard-navigable links/forms.
Ensure each form field has an associated label, appropriate input attributes, and keyboard-accessible error messaging.

requirements.summary: Add required generated logo and hero banner assets and render them in header and hero without layout shifting.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images exist in frontend/public/assets/generated with the specified filenames.
The logo is displayed in the site header/navigation area.
A hero/banner image is displayed in the Home hero section without layout shifting.

file_operations.description: Add the generated logo asset at the exact required path/filename so it can be rendered by the header.
Add the generated hero/banner asset at the exact required path/filename so it can be rendered in the Home hero section.
Render the logo image from frontend/public/assets/generated/jo-enterprises-logo.dim_512x256.png in the header/navigation area with explicit sizing to prevent layout shift.

Render the hero/banner image from frontend/public/assets/generated/jo-enterprises-hero.dim_1600x900.png in the Home hero section with reserved space (explicit dimensions/aspect handling) to prevent layout shift.
